source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

actions_taken: Assistant Driller evacuated to shore by boat on July 5, 2023 at 1:00 am for medical evaluation
BSEE Lake Charles District conducted onsite incident follow-up inspection on July 10, 2023
Second onsite inspection conducted on July 12, 2023
Video footage of incident collected and reviewed
User manual for spinning wrench obtained on July 12, 2023
Gulf Offshore and OIM added second back-up line on July 13, 2023 to prevent wrench rotation exceeding 12 inches
Phone interview conducted with Assistant Driller on July 18, 2023
event.summary: On July 3, 2023, at approximately 3:00 pm, an Assistant Driller on the Enterprise 264 drilling rig was struck in the head by a spinning wrench that rotated clockwise approximately 30 inches while he was standing at the wrench controls. The wrench was not installed according to manufacturer specifications; only one back-up line was attached instead of the required two, allowing excessive rotational movement. The Assistant Driller was evacuated on July 5, 2023 and diagnosed with a concussion. The incident resulted in a required evacuation and injury lasting more than 3 days.
recommendations: Ensure spinning wrench is installed with TWO back-up cables attached to shackles to restrict rotational movement to 12 inches in either direction
Make user manuals readily available to rig floor personnel
Provide written procedures for wrench operation and installation to all rig personnel
Ensure all cautions, warnings, and notes in equipment user manuals are followed during installation and operation
Implement training on proper equipment installation procedures
key_phrases: manufacturer required installation procedure not followed
rig floor personnel trained on the job without written procedures

hazards.name: Mechanical energy from drill pipe spinning operations

threats.name: Inadequate installation of back-up restraint lines
Excessive rotational movement of wrench beyond design limits
Operator positioned in strike zone during wrench operation

controls.name: Installation of two back-up cables to restrict rotational movement
controls.barrier_role: Mechanical restraint to limit wrench rotation
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent excessive rotation

supporting_text: Only one back-up line was installed
A second shackle was attached to the supplied shackle
The wrench rotated clockwise approximately a quarter round, or approximately 30 inches, striking the AD
Step four stated, 'Attach TWO back-up cables to the shackles provided at the rear of the wrench to restrict rotational movement to 12 inches in either direction'

controls.name: User manual availability and accessibility to rig floor personnel
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel have access to installation and operational guidance
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to ensure proper equipment installation through documented procedures

supporting_text: The user manual for the spinning wrench was not readily available to the rig floor personnel
The OIM informed the investigators that rig personnel are trained on the job and not provided with written procedures
The OIM, nor rig personnel, were able to find the user manual during the inspection

controls.name: Written procedures for wrench operation and installation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to standardize equipment installation and operation
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to ensure consistent application of manufacturer requirements

supporting_text: Rig personnel are trained on the job and not provided with written procedures
Warnings, cautions, and notes in the user manual for the spinning wrench were not followed during the installation and operation of the wrench

controls.name: Personnel training on equipment installation and caution/warning compliance
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure personnel understand and follow manufacturer requirements
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to ensure human compliance with engineering requirements

supporting_text: Rig personnel are trained on the job and not provided with written procedures
The user manual for the spinning wrench was not readily available to the rig floor personnel
Warnings, cautions, and notes in the user manual for the spinning wrench were not followed during the installation and operation of the wrench

controls.name: Operator positioning and awareness during wrench operation
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to keep personnel out of strike zone
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary control to reduce consequence severity through operational practices

supporting_text: The wrench rotated toward the AD striking him in the hard hat and head while he was standing at the wrench controls

controls.name: Personal protective equipment (hard hat)
controls.barrier_role: PPE to reduce severity of head injury
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to mitigate injury severity

supporting_text: The wrench rotated toward the AD striking him in the hard hat and head

controls.name: Medical evaluation and evacuation procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure prompt medical assessment and treatment
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to minimize injury consequences through timely medical intervention

supporting_text: On July 4, 2023, at 6:30 am, the AD reported to the EOD Offshore Installation Manager (OIM) that he was not feeling well from the incident the day before
On July 5, 2023, at 1:00 am, Gulf Offshore evacuated the AD to shore by boat for an injury evaluation
At 9:44 am, the Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE), Lake Charles District Office, received an email reporting the evacuation of the AD

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
